title: SolverConfiguration
description:
Complete configuration for the optimization solver.

This structure specifies which algorithm to use, when to stop optimization, algorithm-specific parameters, and logging preferences.

# Example

```no_run use solver_core::models::*;

let config = SolverConfiguration { solver_type: "SimulatedAnnealing".to_string(), stop_conditions: StopConditions { max_iterations: Some(50_000), time_limit_seconds: Some(60), no_improvement_iterations: Some(5_000), }, solver_params: SolverParams::SimulatedAnnealing( SimulatedAnnealingParams { initial_temperature: 100.0, final_temperature: 0.1, cooling_schedule: "geometric".to_string(), reheat_after_no_improvement: Some(0), reheat_cycles: Some(0), } ), logging: LoggingOptions { log_frequency: Some(1000), display_final_schedule: true, log_final_score_breakdown: true, ..Default::default() }, telemetry: Default::default(), seed: None, move_policy: None, allowed_sessions: None, }; ```

allowed_sessions.description: Optional allow-list of session indices that the solver is allowed to modify during iterations. If present, the solver will only generate moves within these sessions, leaving others unchanged. Session indices are 0-based.

logging.description: Logging and output preferences (defaults to minimal logging)

move_policy.description:
Optional move-policy override controlling which move families may run and how they are selected.

When omitted, the solver preserves the current adaptive mixed-search behavior.

seed.description:
Optional seed used to make solver runs reproducible.

When omitted, the solver derives a fresh random seed for the run and reports the effective seed in solver results / benchmark telemetry.

solver_type.description: Type of solver algorithm to use (currently "SimulatedAnnealing")

stop_conditions.description: Conditions that determine when to stop optimization

telemetry.description:
Telemetry options controlling what is emitted via progress updates.

Defaults to disabled to avoid any performance overhead unless explicitly requested.

debug_dump_invariant_context.description: When enabled alongside `debug_validate_invariants`, the solver will include detailed context (attempted move description and before/after schedules) in any invariant violation error.

debug_validate_invariants.description: When enabled, the solver performs invariant checks after each applied move. This is expensive and intended only for debugging.

display_final_schedule.description: Whether to display the final schedule in a human-readable format

log_duration_and_score.description: Whether to log the total optimization time and final score

log_final_score_breakdown.description: Whether to log a detailed breakdown of the final score

log_frequency.description: How often to log progress (every N iterations). `None` disables progress logging.

log_initial_score_breakdown.description: Whether to log a detailed breakdown of the initial score

log_initial_state.description: Whether to log the initial state and configuration

log_stop_condition.description: Whether to log the reason why optimization stopped

MoveFamily.description: Explicit move families supported by the simulated annealing search loop.

MoveFamilyWeights.description: Explicit weights for each move family when `MoveSelectionMode::Weighted` is used.

MovePolicy.description: Controls which move families are allowed in a run and how the solver chooses between them.

allowed_families.description:
Optional allow-list of move families for this run.

When omitted, all move families are allowed.

forced_family.description:
Optional single-family override used for path tests and diagnostics.

When present, the search loop will only attempt the specified move family.

weights.description: Optional explicit family weights used when `mode` is `weighted`.

MoveSelectionMode.description: Selection mode for mixed move-family search.

oneOf.description: Preserve the current solver behavior, where transfer / clique swap probabilities are derived from the current state.
Use caller-provided explicit weights instead of the adaptive heuristics.

SolverParams.description:
Algorithm-specific parameters for different solver types.

This enum allows different algorithms to have their own parameter structures while maintaining a unified API.

oneOf.description: Parameters for the Simulated Annealing algorithm

cooling_schedule.description: Temperature reduction schedule: "geometric" for exponential decay, "linear" for linear decay

final_temperature.description: Ending temperature (lower values focus on local improvements)

initial_temperature.description: Starting temperature (higher values allow more random moves initially)

reheat_after_no_improvement.description:
Optional reheat threshold: number of iterations without improvement before reheating. When reached, temperature is reset to initial_temperature and the cooling schedule is recalculated for the remaining iterations.

Semantics: - `Some(0)`: disable reheating explicitly - `Some(N>0)`: reheat after N iterations without improvement - `None` (unspecified): default to the smaller of `max_iterations/10` or `no_improvement_iterations/2` (if set)

reheat_cycles.description:
Fixed reheat cycles: split the total iterations into this many cycles. For each cycle, temperature cools from `initial_temperature` down to `final_temperature`, then reheats to `initial_temperature` at the cycle boundary.

Semantics: - `Some(0)` or `None`: disabled (use default behavior) - `Some(N>0)`: enable cycle-based reheating with N cycles across `max_iterations`

max_iterations.description: Maximum number of optimization iterations before stopping

no_improvement_iterations.description: Stop if no improvement found for this many iterations

time_limit_seconds.description: Maximum time in seconds before stopping

TelemetryOptions.description:
Controls optional telemetry emitted during solver execution.

This is intentionally separate from `LoggingOptions` so that progress/visualization features can be enabled independently of stdout logging.

best_schedule_every_n_callbacks.description:
Include a schedule snapshot every N progress callbacks (when enabled).

Values <= 1 mean \"every callback\".

emit_best_schedule.description:
When true, include a `best_schedule` snapshot in some progress updates.

This can be expensive for large problems (it clones and serializes schedules), so it is disabled by default.
